# Lucid UI

> Shared React component library for Pandahrms frontend projects. Built with Tailwind CSS v4 (OKLCH color space) and Radix UI primitives. Distributed as the `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` npm package on **GitHub Packages**.

## Installation

The package is published to **GitHub Packages**, not the public npm registry. Consumers need a project-level `.npmrc`:

```
@pandaworks-sw:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com
//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}
```

…and a `GITHUB_TOKEN` env var (classic PAT with `read:packages` scope) on every machine and CI job that runs `pnpm install`.

```bash
pnpm add @pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui
```

Then import named components:

```tsx
import { Button, Badge, Modal, StatCard, MeterRow } from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui';
```

Peer dependencies the consumer must already have: `react >=19`, `react-dom >=19`, `lucide-react >=0.500`, `react-hook-form >=7`, `tailwindcss >=4`. The package ships its own design-token stylesheet — consumers wire it up in their entry CSS:

```css
@import "tailwindcss";
@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css";

@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui";
```

The `styles.css` import provides every token (`--primary`, `--brand`, `--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--muted`, etc.), the base layer, and the `text-display-*` / `text-mono-*` / `bg-pattern-*` utilities. The `@source` line lets Tailwind v4 scan the package's compiled JSX so class names used inside components survive tree-shaking. Fonts (Inter, Comfortaa, JetBrains Mono) must be loaded separately by the consumer.

Tree-shaking works out of the box because the package ships ESM with `sideEffects: false`.

### Customizing components

Customizations land upstream in the lucid-ui repo. Don't fork a single component locally — open a PR against lucid-ui or wrap the component in a project-local component that lives outside of `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`'s exports.

## Tech Stack

- React 19, TypeScript
- Tailwind CSS v4 (OKLCH color space)
- Radix UI primitives
- class-variance-authority for variants
- Lucide icons

## Component Catalog

For full API docs, props, and code examples, read the linked doc file.

### Layout

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| AppShell | Data-driven app layout with collapsible sidebar, header, user menu | [app-shell.md](docs/app-shell.md) |
| PageHeader | Page title, description, and action buttons | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| DetailPage | Resource detail/show page layout with sidebar | [detail-page.md](docs/detail-page.md) |
| EmptyState | Centered empty state with icon, title, description, action | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Modal | Form modal with sticky header/footer, size variants | [modal.md](docs/modal.md) |
| Stepper | Multi-step progress (horizontal or vertical); descriptions only on current step | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Separator | Horizontal or vertical divider | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |
| Breadcrumb | Navigation breadcrumb trail | [layout.md](docs/layout.md) |

### Forms

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Button | Variants, action presets, icon, tooltip, loading | [button.md](docs/button.md) |
| Input | Text input field | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Label | Accessible form label | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Textarea | Multi-line text input | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Checkbox | Checkbox with label support | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| RadioGroup | Radio button group | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Switch | Toggle switch | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Select | Static dropdown (< 10 items) | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| SelectPicker | Searchable dropdown, single/multi select | [select-picker.md](docs/select-picker.md) |
| DatePicker | Single date picker | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| DateRangePicker | Date range picker with presets | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Slider | Range slider | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| Form | react-hook-form integration with field context | [form.md](docs/form.md) |
| SearchInput | Debounced search input with clear button | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| SplitButton | Primary action + dropdown secondary actions | [split-button.md](docs/split-button.md) |
| AttachmentInput | File upload with dropzone/compact variants | [attachment-input.md](docs/attachment-input.md) |
| SelectableCard | Card-based single/multi selection | [forms.md](docs/forms.md) |
| FilterBar | FilterButton trigger + ActiveFilters chips | [filter-bar.md](docs/filter-bar.md) |

### Data Display

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Card | Container with header, content, footer | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Table | Data table with header, body, footer | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Badge | Status indicator with semantic tones (default/secondary/destructive/outline/success/warning/info/muted) and optional dot | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Avatar | User/entity avatar with image and fallback; `<AvatarFallback>` auto-converts a full-name string to a 2-char monogram and is colorized by default (opt out via `colorize={false}`); supports `shape="circle"` (default) or `"square"` | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| AvatarGroup | Stack of overlapping Avatars with `max` overflow tile; inherits the Avatar shape | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Calendar | Date calendar | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Skeleton | Loading placeholder | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| Progress | Progress bar | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| StatCard | Dashboard stat tile (Card + AnimatedNumber + Badge) with delta chip | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| MultiStatCard | Two or more related stat metrics inside one card with dividers between cells; horizontal or vertical orientation | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| MeterRow | Labeled horizontal bar with per-row tone (success/warning/info/destructive/muted) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| ListRow | Avatar/icon + title + subtitle + trailing-meta row primitive; supports interactive `asButton` mode | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| CodeLabel | Inline monospace label with optional copy button (`copyable` prop) | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| AnimatedNumber | Animated number display with easing | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |
| ExpandableText | Collapsible long text with show more/less | [data-display.md](docs/data-display.md) |

### Feedback

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Alert | Alert banner (default, destructive, info) | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| AlertDialog | Modal confirmation dialog | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| Sonner | Toast notifications | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| LoadingPage | Full-screen loading state | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| ConnectionBanner | Offline/connectivity warning banner | [feedback.md](docs/feedback.md) |
| ErrorFallback | Error boundary fallback (root/route variants) | [error-fallback.md](docs/error-fallback.md) |

### Overlay

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Dialog | Modal dialog with header, content, footer | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Sheet | Slide-out panel (top, right, bottom, left) | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| DropdownMenu | Context/action menu | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Tooltip | Hover tooltip | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Popover | Click-triggered popover | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |
| Command | Command palette with search | [overlay.md](docs/overlay.md) |

### Navigation

| Component | Description | Docs |
|-----------|-------------|------|
| Tabs | Tab panels (pill and line variants) | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Accordion | Collapsible sections | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Collapsible | Single collapsible section | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Pagination | Page navigation | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ScrollArea | Custom scrollbar container | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| Toggle | Single toggle button | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |
| ToggleGroup | Group of toggle buttons | [navigation.md](docs/navigation.md) |

## Decision Guide: Which Component to Use

For **variant / tone / surface / size choices** within a chosen component (e.g. which Badge tone? Modal vs Dialog vs Sheet? `default` vs `sm` Button? human → circle vs entity → square Avatar?), see [decision-guide.md](docs/decision-guide.md). The table below picks the *component*; the linked doc picks the *variant*.

| Scenario | Component |
|----------|-----------|
| App-wide layout with sidebar nav | AppShell |
| Page title + description + actions | PageHeader |
| Detail/show page for a resource | DetailPage |
| Form modal with sticky header/footer | Modal |
| Short static dropdown (< 10 items) | Select |
| Searchable dropdown, long lists | SelectPicker |
| Multiple selection from a list | SelectPicker (mode="multiple") |
| Pick a single date | DatePicker |
| Pick a date range | DateRangePicker |
| File upload with drag & drop | AttachmentInput (variant="dropzone") |
| Inline file upload button | AttachmentInput (variant="compact") |
| Card-based single/multi selection | SelectableCard |
| Standard action (create/edit/delete) | Button (action prop) |
| Primary + secondary actions | SplitButton |
| Search/filter input with debounce | SearchInput |
| Table filtering with chips | FilterButton + ActiveFilters |
| Multi-step wizard progress | Stepper |
| Animated score/metric display | AnimatedNumber |
| Collapsible long text | ExpandableText |
| Confirmation before destructive action | AlertDialog |
| Form modal / side panel | Dialog or Sheet |
| Inline monospace ID with copy | CodeLabel |
| Toast notifications | Sonner (toast) |
| Status indicator | Badge |
| Loading placeholder | Skeleton |
| Full-screen loading state | LoadingPage |
| Offline/connectivity warning | ConnectionBanner |
| Error boundary fallback | ErrorFallback |

## Styling Conventions

- Always support dark mode via the `.dark` class (set on `<html>` or an ancestor) plus `dark:` Tailwind variants
- All theme colors are CSS variables defined in `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css` (stone neutrals + panda-blue accent + semantic success/info/warning/danger)
- Color space: OKLCH for `bg-pattern-*` utilities; design tokens use HEX for portability
- Icons: Lucide React (`lucide-react`)
- Fonts: Inter (sans), Comfortaa (display), JetBrains Mono (mono) — load via Google Fonts in your `index.html`

## Changelog

Machine-readable changelog for AI agents consuming this library.
When upgrading `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` in a downstream project, use this section to determine what changed and what actions are required.

Each entry contains:
- **component**: the component name
- **change**: what changed (added | modified | removed | renamed)
- **action**: what consuming projects must do
- **breaking**: whether this is a breaking change
- **details**: concise description of the change

---

### 2026-05-02

#### package — npm package renamed to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: The npm package was renamed from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui` so the package name matches the `lucid-ui` repo it ships from. The previous `@pandaworks-sw/ui` package on GitHub Packages stops receiving updates at v0.1.4; v0.2.0 is the first release under the new name. No source code, exports, public API, or styling tokens change — only the package name and subpath import paths.
- action:
  - In `package.json`, replace `"@pandaworks-sw/ui": "<old>"` with `"@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui": "^0.2.0"`.
  - Rewrite every import: `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` → `from '@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui'`. Stylesheet too: `@import "@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css"` → `@import "@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui/styles.css"`.
  - Update Tailwind v4 source line: `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/ui"` → `@source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui"`.
  - `.npmrc` is unchanged — the scope `@pandaworks-sw` is the same; only the package within the scope changed.
  - Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

#### multi-stat-card — new component

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `MultiStatCard` component renders 2+ related stat metrics inside a single card with dividers between cells. Each cell mirrors `StatCard` (icon + label, animated headline value, optional hint and delta chip) and reuses the same design tokens, headline auto-shrink heuristic, and delta-chip rendering. Per-cell shape is `MultiStatCardItem` (subset of `StatCardProps` minus `className`): `icon`, `label`, `value`, `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, `formatter`, `hint`, `delta`, `deltaTone`. `orientation` (default `"horizontal"`) controls divider direction (`divide-x` vs `divide-y`); cells use `flex-1 min-w-0` so widths split evenly and long values still shrink. Use it for funnel metrics (Total / In Progress / Completed), date pairs (Form Start / Submission Due), or any "metrics that belong together" cluster you don't want broken across separate tiles. Internally, the shared helpers (`DELTA_VARIANT`, `DELTA_ICON`, `getHeadlineSizeClass`, `getRenderedLength`, `DeltaTone`) were extracted from `stat-card.tsx` into a new sibling `stat-card/stat-card-shared.ts` so both components stay in sync — no behavior change for existing `StatCard` usage.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace ad-hoc multi-cell `Card`s (e.g. a `Card` containing a 3-column grid of label/number divs) with `<MultiStatCard items={[...]} />`
  - When mixing into a grid of single `StatCard` tiles, give the `MultiStatCard` a `col-span` equal to its cell count so the row stays balanced (e.g. `className="sm:col-span-2 xl:col-span-3"` for a 3-cell card inside an `xl:grid-cols-6` grid)

#### stat-card — `value` prop now accepts strings

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `StatCard`'s `value` prop is widened from `number` to `number | string`. Numeric values keep the existing `AnimatedNumber` path (with `prefix` / `suffix` / `decimals` / `formatter` adornments). String values render as-is — useful for already-formatted or non-numeric tiles such as `"N/A"`, `"—"`, `"Active"`, `"Enterprise"`, `"8 / 10"`, or pre-formatted currency. `prefix`, `suffix`, `decimals`, and `formatter` are ignored when `value` is a string (strings are assumed already-formatted). The `delta` / `deltaTone` chip and `hint` continue to work for both forms.
- action:
  - No migration needed for existing numeric usage.
  - When showing a non-animated label (status, tier, ratio, "N/A"), pass the string directly instead of building a custom `Card` wrapper.

#### stat-card — headline auto-shrinks for long values

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: The headline class steps down based on the rendered value's length so long values still fit inside the tile without overflowing or wrapping awkwardly: `≤10 chars → text-xl`, `≤16 → text-lg`, `≤24 → text-base`, `>24 → text-sm`. Length is computed from the string for string values, from `formatter(value)` when a formatter is provided, and from `prefix + integer digits + decimals + suffix` for plain numbers. Pure CSS — no `ResizeObserver`, no measurement, no extra render — so the cost is zero. The headline wrapper also gets `min-w-0` so it can shrink inside narrower grid columns. Short numeric values (the common case, e.g. `value={42}` or `value={73} suffix="%"`) keep `text-xl` and look identical to before.
- action:
  - No migration needed.
  - If a value is now appearing smaller than expected, count the rendered length (string, or `prefix + digits + decimals + suffix`) and compare against the thresholds. If the value is supposed to be a hero number, the tile is the wrong primitive — use a plain `Card` with custom typography.

### 2026-05-01

#### avatar — `getInitialName` utility + `AvatarFallback` auto-converts full names

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New helper `getInitialName(name: string): string` exported from `@pandaworks-sw/ui` (and from the registry's internal `@/lib` alias — see `packages/registry/src/lib/get-initial-name.ts`). Pass any string — including a full multi-word name — to `<AvatarFallback>` and it now renders a 2-character monogram automatically: `"John Doe" → "JD"`, `"Devi Marasinghe" → "DM"` (first letter of first + last word), `"Eli" → "EL"` (first 2 chars of single-word names), `""` → empty. Already-short strings such as `"JD"` or `"+3"` pass through unchanged so existing call sites that pre-compute initials keep working. Callers that previously hand-wrote a `getInitials` helper (e.g. `app-shell.tsx`) can drop it and pass `user.name` directly.
- action:
  - Optional cleanup: replace ad-hoc `getInitials(user.name)` helpers with `<AvatarFallback>{user.name}</AvatarFallback>` or `import { getInitialName } from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'` for non-Avatar contexts (aria-labels, sorting).

#### avatar — `AvatarFallback` colorize default flipped to `true`

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: The `colorize` prop on `AvatarFallback` now defaults to `true`. `<AvatarFallback>John Doe</AvatarFallback>` renders a deterministically colored monogram out of the box (A–Z map to 26 evenly spaced OKLCH hues; digits and other characters fall back to a deterministic hash; foreground is white in both light and dark mode). Pass `colorize={false}` to opt back into the muted (`bg-muted`) look — useful for `+N` overflow tiles, system placeholders, and other non-name fallbacks. The `+N` overflow tile inside `AvatarGroup` (`packages/registry/registry/default/avatar-group/avatar-group.tsx`) was updated to set `colorize={false}` so it keeps its neutral background.
- action:
  - If a project relied on the previous gray default for arbitrary, non-name text content (e.g. `"+N"` chips, system tiles, single-character placeholders), add `colorize={false}` to those `AvatarFallback` instances.
  - Name-based avatars need no change — they get the colorized look automatically.
  - The previous opt-in form `<AvatarFallback colorize>JD</AvatarFallback>` still works (just redundant now).

#### registry — `@/lib` is now a folder alias inside the demo

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `apps/demo/vite.config.ts` replaces the file-specific `@/lib/utils` alias with a folder-level `@/lib` alias pointing at `packages/registry/src/lib/`. Existing `import { cn } from '@/lib/utils'` continues to resolve via file lookup inside the aliased folder. The new preferred form for registry source files is `import { cn, getInitialName } from '@/lib'` — backed by a new `packages/registry/src/lib/index.ts` barrel.
- action: nothing required for downstream consumers — they import from `@pandaworks-sw/ui`, not from `@/lib`. The change only affects internal registry source files.

#### app-shell — handle small screens (mobile view)

- change: fixed
- breaking: no
- details: Three mobile/compact-viewport UX bugs in `packages/registry/registry/default/app-shell/app-shell.tsx`. (1) The mobile Sheet sidebar did not auto-close when the user tapped a nav link, the branding link, or a user-action link — the route changed but the Sheet stayed mounted, forcing manual dismissal. Each link now calls `setOpenMobile(false)` on click via a small `useMobileAutoClose` hook (no-op on desktop). (2) The header content area lacked `min-w-0` / `shrink-0` on its flex children, so a wide consumer `header` (e.g. a search input with long placeholder) wrapped or pushed `navbarActions` off-screen at narrow widths instead of shrinking. The header wrapper, the header content slot, the sidebar trigger, the separator, and the navbar-actions slot now have explicit `min-w-0` / `shrink-0` so consumer content shrinks gracefully. (3) The full 18rem sidebar squeezed the navbar at compact desktop widths (768–1023px) — for example, the saas demo's search input was truncated to "Searc…" at 768px. AppShell now controls `SidebarProvider`'s `open` state via the new `useIsCompactDesktop` hook (`packages/registry/src/hooks/use-mobile.ts`); the sidebar collapses to icon mode automatically inside the compact range and expands past 1024px. Mobile (<768px) keeps using the Sheet variant.
- action: nothing required for consumers. Existing `linkComponent` integrations keep working because the auto-close handler is attached as `onClick` and is forwarded through the consumer's link via spread. Apps that pass a wide custom `header` should still apply their own internal `truncate` / `min-w-0` to long text — AppShell now allows the slot to shrink, but the consumer is responsible for what happens inside it. The compact-viewport auto-collapse is automatic — AppShell no longer accepts a `defaultOpen` prop, but consumers who need controlled-open behavior can still wrap their own `SidebarProvider`. The new `useIsCompactDesktop` hook is also re-exported via the app-shell barrel for consumers wanting matching responsive logic in their own headers.

#### design-token stylesheet now ships with the package

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: The package now exposes `@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css` as a subpath export. The file contains the full design system the components rely on — `:root` / `.dark` token blocks, the `@theme inline` mapping that produces Tailwind v4 utilities (`bg-primary`, `text-brand`, `bg-stone-200`, `text-panda-500`, etc.), the base layer (border-color reset, fonts on `html` / headings / `code`), the `text-display-{sm,md,lg,xl}` and `text-mono-{sm,md}` typography utilities, and the `bg-pattern-*` background utilities (dots, grid, cross, noise, aurora + 9 aurora colour variants). Source of truth: `packages/registry/src/styles.css`. Build pipeline: `tsup` `onSuccess` hook copies the file into `dist/styles.css` on every build; `package.json` `exports` adds `"./styles.css": "./dist/styles.css"`. The demo's `apps/demo/src/index.css` was reduced to four lines and now imports the same file directly so demo and package can never drift.
- action:
  - Replace the hand-copied token CSS in your app's entry stylesheet with:
    ```css
    @import "tailwindcss";
    @import "@pandaworks-sw/ui/styles.css";
    @source "../node_modules/@pandaworks-sw/ui";
    ```
  - The `@source` line is required so Tailwind v4 scans the package's compiled JSX for class names — without it, utilities used inside `@pandaworks-sw/ui` components will be tree-shaken out of your CSS bundle.
  - Fonts are not bundled: keep loading Inter, Comfortaa, and JetBrains Mono yourself (Google Fonts `<link>` in `index.html` is fine; see `apps/demo/index.html`).

#### unused build output, scripts, and configs removed

- change: removed
- breaking: yes
- details: With the npm package on GitHub Packages now the only distribution channel, the project drops the legacy build artefacts that produced a separate JSON-based component output. Files deleted: `public/r/` (61 component JSONs), `packages/registry/registry.json`, the per-workspace `components.json` files, `packages/registry/scripts/rewrite-lucid-namespace.mjs`, `packages/registry/scripts/sync-all-meta.mjs`, `packages/registry/registry/default/_all/`, and `apps/demo/src/components/install-command.tsx`. Configs simplified: root `package.json` drops the `registry:build` script and reworks `build` to run `@pandaworks-sw/ui build:lib && @pandaworks-sw/demo build`; `packages/registry/package.json` drops `registry:build`, simplifies `build` to `tsup`, and removes the `shadcn` devDep; `biome.json` drops the `!public/r` ignore entries; `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` no longer copies `public/r` into the Pages artifact; `apps/demo/src/showcase/component-page.tsx` and `showcase-app.tsx` drop the `<InstallCommand />` widget and its `installName` prop. Documentation in `CLAUDE.md` and `README.md` updated accordingly.
- action:
  - For new projects: install with `pnpm add @pandaworks-sw/ui` after configuring the `.npmrc` for GitHub Packages (see Installation above).
  - For existing apps still vendoring `src/components/lucid/`: migrate by adding `@pandaworks-sw/ui` as a dependency, running a codemod to rewrite `from '@/components/lucid/<name>'` → `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'`, and deleting the `src/components/lucid/` folder.

#### npm package scope rename + GitHub Packages distribution

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: The npm package was renamed from `@lucid/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/ui` and is now published to **GitHub Packages** (not the public npm registry). The demo workspace was likewise renamed from `@lucid/demo` to `@pandaworks-sw/demo`. GitHub Packages requires the npm scope to match the GitHub org owning the repository, so the repo is moving to `github.com/pandaworks-sw/lucid-ui`. A new release workflow at `.github/workflows/publish.yml` publishes the package whenever a GitHub Release is published.
- action:
  - Add an `.npmrc` at the project root with `@pandaworks-sw:registry=https://npm.pkg.github.com` and `//npm.pkg.github.com/:_authToken=${GITHUB_TOKEN}`. Set `GITHUB_TOKEN` to a classic PAT with `read:packages` scope on every developer machine and CI job. Update `package.json` from `@lucid/ui` to `@pandaworks-sw/ui`, then rewrite all `from '@lucid/ui'` imports to `from '@pandaworks-sw/ui'`. Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

#### rebrand (Pandaworks UI → Lucid UI)

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: Library rebranded from `Pandaworks UI` to `Lucid UI`. The npm package was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/components` to `@lucid/ui`. The demo workspace was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/demo` to `@lucid/demo`. The GitHub repo was renamed from `pandaworks-software-plt/pandaworks-ui` to `pandaworks-software-plt/lucid-ui`; GitHub auto-redirects the old URL. Internal-only library — no external consumers were affected.
- action:
  - Update `package.json` from `@pandaworks-ui/components` to `@lucid/ui`, then update all `from '@pandaworks-ui/components'` imports to `from '@lucid/ui'`

#### npm-package distribution

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: Component sources now publish as the `@pandaworks-ui/components` npm package. The previously private `packages/registry/` workspace package was renamed from `@pandaworks-ui/registry` to `@pandaworks-ui/components`, made public, and given a proper `exports` map (`./dist/index.js` ESM + `./dist/index.d.ts`). New build artifacts: [packages/registry/src/index.ts](packages/registry/src/index.ts) re-exports all 59 component folders + hooks (`useCopyToClipboard`, plus `useIsMobile` via the `app-shell` barrel) + the `cn` util; [packages/registry/tsup.config.ts](packages/registry/tsup.config.ts) emits ESM + DTS to `packages/registry/dist/` (185 KB ESM, 56 KB types, sourcemaps, tree-shakeable with `sideEffects: false`). Peer deps: `react >=19`, `react-dom >=19`, `lucide-react >=0.500`, `react-hook-form >=7`, `tailwindcss >=4`.
- action:
  - For new Pandahrms apps: `pnpm add @pandaworks-ui/components` and `import { Button, Badge, … } from '@pandaworks-ui/components'`
  - Tailwind v4 must already be configured in the consumer with the design tokens this library expects (`--primary`, `--brand`, `--success`, `--info`, `--warning`, `--muted`, etc.). See `apps/demo/src/index.css` for the canonical token set

#### empty-state, list-row (TypeScript fixes)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `EmptyState` and `ListRow` both define a `title: ReactNode` prop that previously conflicted with the inherited HTML `title?: string` attribute. `EmptyState` now uses `Omit<HTMLAttributes<HTMLDivElement>, "title">` on its base type. `ListRow`'s internal spread casts (`rest as ButtonProps` / `rest as DivProps`) now use `Omit<…, keyof SharedListRowProps>` so the rest object no longer re-introduces `title` after destructuring. No public API change. Existing usage continues to compile and render the same. The fix unblocks the demo build (and the GitHub Pages deploy job), which was failing with `TS2430` / `TS2322`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the typed-correctly versions

#### split-button (new brand variant)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `SplitButton` gains a `brand` variant matching `Button`'s brand styling (`bg-brand text-brand-foreground shadow-xs hover:shadow-sm`) plus a corresponding entry in `dividerClasses` (`border-l border-brand-foreground/20`). Closes a parity gap surfaced by the pure-showcase Projects page, which already used `<SplitButton variant="brand">` for its "New project" CTA but compiled-failed because the variant didn't exist. Existing variants (`default`, `destructive`, `outline`, `secondary`, `ghost`) are unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the brand variant
  - Use `<SplitButton variant="brand">` for primary "create"-style split actions; pairs visually with `<Button action="create">`

#### stat-card

- change: modified
- breaking: yes (visual)
- details: `StatCard` is now a single SaaS-density size — tight padding (`px-4`), `text-xs` label, `size-3.5` icon, and a `text-xl` headline number. The previous `text-3xl` headline ate too much screen estate inside dashboards. No API change: the `size` prop introduced earlier today was removed before any consumer adopted it. Existing `<StatCard … />` usage continues to compile but renders smaller; tighten any surrounding grid gaps (`gap-4` → `gap-3`) if the layout looks too sparse.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new sizing
  - Audit pages that use `StatCard` — the visual is smaller, so previously cramped dashboards will breathe but very sparse layouts may need extra tiles or tighter grid gaps

#### attachment-input, button, split-button, pagination, breadcrumb

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Five components now set `position: relative` on the root that wraps an `sr-only` accessibility node. Tailwind's `sr-only` utility uses `position: absolute`; without a positioned ancestor it falls through to the initial containing block (the viewport) and lays out at its static offset, stretching the document height. Pages that rendered several of these components (the showcase being the worst case) could scroll past the bottom into empty space. Affected roots: `AttachmentInput` wrapper, `buttonVariants` (Button) base, `splitButtonVariants` (SplitButton) base, `PaginationEllipsis`, `BreadcrumbEllipsis`. The `Button` change also fixes consumers like `FilterButton` and `SidebarTrigger` that nest `<span className="sr-only">…</span>` inside a `<Button>`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the fix
  - No prop or API changes; existing usage works as-is

#### avatar

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `AvatarFallback` gains an optional `colorize` boolean prop. When passed, the background color is derived from the first character of the fallback text — A–Z map to 26 evenly spaced OKLCH hues (`oklch(0.62 0.14 hue)`), digits and other characters fall back to a deterministic hash. Foreground is set to white. The default `bg-muted` fallback styling is unchanged when `colorize` is not set.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new prop
  - Opt in per usage: `<AvatarFallback colorize>JD</AvatarFallback>`
  - Custom `className` / `style` still take precedence over the computed colors

#### table

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Table` wrapper drops its own border, rounded corners, `bg-card`, and shadow when nested inside `CardContent`, so the surrounding card frame is the only visual boundary (fixes "card within a card" appearance). Standalone `Table` usage is unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the nesting-aware wrapper. `CardContent` now sets `data-slot="card-content"`, which is required for the table to detect the card context

#### card

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CardContent` now sets `data-slot="card-content"` on its root element so descendant components (e.g. `Table`) can adapt their styling when nested inside.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required in consumers

#### badge

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Badge` gains four semantic-tone variants — `success` / `warning` / `info` / `muted` — keyed off the existing `--success` / `--info` / `--warning` / `--muted` design tokens, and a new `dot` boolean prop that renders a leading status dot tinted to match the variant. Default visual for the original four variants (`default`, `secondary`, `destructive`, `outline`) is unchanged. Closes registry gap G1.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new variants
  - Replace bespoke status pills (`<span className="bg-emerald-50 text-emerald-700 …">`) with `<Badge variant="success" dot>Active</Badge>`

#### avatar

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `Avatar` accepts a new `shape` prop (`"circle"` (default) or `"square"`). Square avatars use `rounded-md` and inherit through `AvatarFallback` so the fallback layer stays in sync (e.g. while an image loads). Useful for project tiles, app icons, and other entity avatars where round + person doesn't fit. Closes registry gap G2. The previously shipped `colorize` prop on `AvatarFallback` is unchanged.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing usage is unchanged; opt in via `<Avatar shape="square">`

#### avatar-group

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `AvatarGroup` component that horizontally stacks `Avatar` children with negative spacing (`-space-x-1` / `-space-x-2` / `-space-x-3`), an outline ring against the page background, and a `+N` overflow tile when more avatars are passed than the `max` prop. Inherits the per-Avatar `shape` (or accepts a group-level `shape` prop). Sizes: `xs` / `sm` / `md` / `lg`. Closes registry gap G3.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Wrap your existing avatar stacks: `<AvatarGroup max={3} size="md">{members.map(…)}</AvatarGroup>`

#### empty-state

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `EmptyState` component for filtered tables/lists, "no results" panels, and any centered "nothing to show" surface. Slots for `icon`, `title`, `description`, and an `action`. Three sizes (`sm` / `md` / `lg`) controlling vertical padding and icon-tile size. Wraps the icon in a soft muted disc. Closes registry gap G4.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace hand-rolled `<div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-3 py-16 …">` empty blocks with `<EmptyState icon={…} title="…" action={…} />`

#### stat-card

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `StatCard` component for dashboard tiles. Composes registry primitives (`Card`, `AnimatedNumber`, `Badge`) and accepts: `icon`, `label`, `value`, `suffix`, `hint`, `delta`, `deltaTone` (`"up"` / `"down"` / `"flat"`). The delta chip resolves to a Badge `success` / `destructive` / `muted` variant with the appropriate trend icon (`TrendingUp` / `TrendingDown` / `Minus`). Closes registry gap G5.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing dashboard stat cards collapse from ~30 lines of Card + custom delta chip to a single `<StatCard …>`

#### dropdown-menu

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `DropdownMenuItem` accepts a new `variant` prop (`"default"` (default) or `"destructive"`). When set to `"destructive"`, the item text and any nested icon use `text-destructive`, and the focus state shifts to a tinted destructive background (`focus:bg-destructive/10`). Replaces the manual `className="text-destructive focus:text-destructive"` pattern. Closes registry gap G9.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace `<DropdownMenuItem className="text-destructive focus:text-destructive">` with `<DropdownMenuItem variant="destructive">`

#### code-label

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CodeLabel` accepts a new `copyable` boolean prop (default `true`). Pass `copyable={false}` for a static inline code chip with no copy affordance — useful when the label is purely visual (e.g. listing many project keys in a budget summary table). Existing usage is unchanged. Closes registry gap G8.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Existing usage is unchanged; opt in via `<CodeLabel value={key} copyable={false} />`

#### meter-row

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `MeterRow` component — labeled horizontal bar with optional trailing value and per-row tone (`default`, `success`, `warning`, `info`, `destructive`, `muted`). Sizes (`sm` / `default` / `lg`). Replaces the hand-rolled `<div className="h-1.5 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-muted"><div style={{ width: \`${pct}%\` }} />` pattern that was repeated across `Dashboard.DepartmentChart` and the `Reports` status / priority charts. Closes registry gap G6.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace hand-rolled labeled bars with `<MeterRow label={…} value={n} max={total} valueLabel={n} tone="success" />`

#### list-row

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `ListRow` component — `leading + title + subtitle + trailing` layout primitive used across the SaaS demo's activity feeds, deadline lists, team lists, and API key lists. Pass `asButton` to render as a keyboard-accessible button with focus ring; otherwise renders as a div. Density variants (`sm` / `default` / `lg`) and surface variants (`plain` / `bordered` / `muted`). Closes registry gap G7.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Collapse manual `<div className="flex items-center gap-3 …">` row layouts into `<ListRow leading={…} title={…} subtitle={…} trailing={…} />`

#### decision-guide

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New `public/docs/decision-guide.md` is the canonical "which variant / tone / surface / size?" reference. Covers Avatar shape (human → `circle`, non-human entity → `square`), Badge tones with the `dot` rule, Alert variants, MeterRow tones, ListRow surface, Tabs `line` vs pill, StatCard `deltaTone` (tone follows desirability, not arrow direction), Modal vs Dialog vs Sheet vs AlertDialog, text-input choice (Input / Textarea / SearchInput / CodeLabel), dropdown choice (Select for static < 10 / SelectPicker otherwise), date controls, boolean / multi-choice rules (Switch = immediate effect; Checkbox = collected on Save), AttachmentInput dropzone vs compact, dashboard tile choice (StatCard / MeterRow / ListRow / Card), the registry's fixed elevation ladder (`shadow-none` → `shadow-xs/sm` → `shadow-md` → `shadow-xl`) with an explicit note that `Card` has no `elevation` prop and is fixed at `shadow-xs` by design, DropdownMenuItem `default` vs `destructive`, and labelled vs icon-only Button rules. The Decision Guide section in `public/llms.txt` now links to it.
- action: no consumer action — read this when picking a variant/tone/surface

### 2026-04-23

#### tooltip

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `TooltipContent` now wraps Radix `Portal` so the popper layer mounts on `document.body`, avoiding clipping and stacking issues inside scrollable regions or transformed layout (same pattern as `PopoverContent`).
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the change
  - No API changes; custom `className` / props on `TooltipContent` behave as before

#### stepper

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Smaller circles (`size-7`) and labels; completed steps and finished track segments use emerald (`emerald-600` / `emerald-500` in dark). Current step uses primary border plus a light ring. New optional `orientation` prop: `"vertical"` renders a top-down list with a left spine. Horizontal layout keeps leading/trailing `flex-1` segments for even spacing. Step `description` and optional `tooltip` popover render only when that step is the current index. Track segments layer a muted base with an emerald fill that animates with `scale-x` / `scale-y` (~500ms, `ease-out`); `prefers-reduced-motion` removes the transition.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up visuals and `orientation`
  - Override `className` if you need larger step markers than the new defaults

#### code-label

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `CodeLabel` now uses a subtler filled background (no outline border). The copy button is hidden by default and reveals with width/opacity expansion on hover/focus, appears on touch/press for mobile, and stays visible briefly after touch release and copy success feedback.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to get the updated behavior and styling
  - No API changes; existing `value`, `size`, and `className` usage stays the same

#### sheet

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Sheet panels are inset from the viewport (floating card), use rounded corners (`rounded-2xl`), `shadow-xl`, a light ring, and `overflow-y-auto` with overscroll containment. No enter/exit animation classes on overlay or content (Radix still handles mount/unmount timing).
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up styles
  - If you relied on edge-to-edge sheets, override `className` on `SheetContent` to restore full-bleed layout

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `brand`, `destructive`, `outline`, and `secondary` variants now use `shadow-xs` with `hover:shadow-sm` instead of bespoke multi-layer inset/box shadows. Visual weight is slightly flatter.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` if you want the updated styling
  - No prop or API changes

#### dialog

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `DialogOverlay` uses `bg-black/50` with `backdrop-blur-[1px]`. `DialogContent` adds `border-border` and uses `shadow-md` instead of `shadow-lg`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### command

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Root `Command` panel adds `border border-border/70` and `shadow-md`. `CommandGroup` heading styles use Tailwind v4 `**:[[cmdk-group-heading]]:…` descendant variants instead of `[&_[cmdk-group-heading]]:…`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - If you overrode group heading styles via global CSS on `[cmdk-group-heading]`, re-check specificity against the new utilities

#### dropdown-menu

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Content/sub-content use `border-border`, `min-w-32`, and `shadow-md` on sub-content. Items use `data-disabled:` variant selectors instead of `data-[disabled]:`. `max-h` uses `max-h-(--radix-dropdown-menu-content-available-height)`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### select

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `SelectTrigger` uses `shadow-xs` / `focus:shadow-sm` and `data-placeholder:text-muted-foreground`. `SelectContent` uses `min-w-32`, `shadow-sm`, and `data-disabled:` on items. Popper viewport uses `h-(--radix-select-trigger-height)` and `min-w-(--radix-select-trigger-width)`.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### popover

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `PopoverContent` adds explicit `border-border` alongside existing border and shadow utilities.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`

#### app-shell

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: `AppShell` now uses the inset sidebar variant by default, and the inset main surface uses a larger rounded frame (`rounded-3xl`) with existing border/shadow layering for a blended dashboard shell look.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the updated shell framing
  - No API changes; existing `AppShellProps` usage remains compatible

#### registry batch (alert-dialog, app-shell, attachment-input, card, input, modal, selectable-card, split-button, table, textarea)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Minor className alignment for Tailwind v4 patterns and visual consistency (e.g. `border-border`, shadow tokens, `data-disabled` where Radix exposes the attribute, small layout tokens). No intentional public API changes.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up pixel-identical styling

### 2026-04-08

#### detail-page

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `DetailPageSidebarSection` sub-component. A sidebar section with header row (label + optional action slot) and free-form children. Inspired by GitHub issue detail sidebar pattern.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required. Existing `DetailPageMetaItem` usage is unaffected.
  - New `DetailPageSidebarSection` is available for use alongside or instead of `DetailPageMetaItem`

#### repo rename

- change: renamed
- breaking: yes
- details: Repository renamed from `pandahrms-ui-registry` to `pandaworks-ui`.
- action:
  - Update any references to the old repo URL.

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: `default` variant is now standard shadcn/ui style (plain). Previous `default` (blue with inset shadows) moved to new `brand` variant.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the new defaults
  - Search codebase for `variant="default"` on `<Button>` -- if the intent was the blue styled button, change to `variant="brand"`
  - Buttons with no explicit `variant` prop now render as the plain default -- verify this is acceptable or add `variant="brand"` where the blue button was expected
  - Action presets `create` and `save` already updated to use `brand` -- no change needed if using `action="create"` or `action="save"`

---

### 2026-04-03

#### sonner

- change: modified
- breaking: yes
- details: Decoupled from `next-themes`. No longer imports or depends on ThemeProvider. Improved type safety and accessibility.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - If your project was relying on sonner auto-detecting theme from `next-themes`, you may need to pass the `theme` prop explicitly

#### all components (24 affected)

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `data-slot` attributes for DOM identification. Standardized double quotes. No API changes.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui` to pick up the changes
  - No code changes required in consuming projects

#### barrel exports (19 components)

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: Added `index.ts` barrel exports for avatar, badge, button, calendar, card, checkbox, collapsible, command, dialog, dropdown-menu, input, label, popover, separator, sheet, sidebar, toggle, tooltip, app-shell-types.
- action:
  - No action required. Consuming projects can optionally update imports to use barrel exports

#### useCopyToClipboard

- change: added
- breaking: no
- details: New hook in `src/hooks/use-copy-to-clipboard.ts`. Copies text to clipboard with a reset timer.
- action:
  - Available for use in consuming projects. No action required unless you want to use it.

---

### 2026-03-31

#### button

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Added `action` prop with 12 presets (create, edit, save, delete, cancel, view, export, import, archive, duplicate, print, link). Added `icon`, `tooltip`, and `loading` props. Auto-resolves icon, variant, label, and tooltip from action presets.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - Replace all `<CreateButton>`, `<EditButton>`, `<SaveButton>`, etc. with `<Button action="create">`, `<Button action="edit">`, `<Button action="save">`, etc.
  - Replace `<ConfirmableActionButton>` with `<AlertDialog>` wrapping `<Button action="delete">`
  - Remove all imports from `@/components/ui/action-button/*`

#### action-button

- change: removed
- breaking: yes
- details: Entire component removed (15 files). All functionality consolidated into Button `action` prop.
- action:
  - Delete `@/components/ui/action-button/` directory from consuming projects
  - See button entry above for migration steps

#### alert-dialog

- change: modified
- breaking: no
- details: Internal refactor -- AlertDialog action/cancel buttons now use `<Button>` via `asChild` instead of applying `buttonVariants` classes directly.
- action:
  - Upgrade `@pandaworks-sw/ui`
  - No code changes required in consuming projects
